title: Anathallis maguirei
taxonomy: Anathallis maguirei is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This family consists of approximately 30,000 known species of orchids, making it one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. Anathallis maguirei is specifically a member of the subtribe Pleurothallidinae, which is a group of terrestrial and epiphytic orchids commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The subtribe Pleurothallidinae is further divided into several genera, with Anathallis being one of them. Anathallis maguirei is a type of orchid that is typically found in the Americas, with a specific distribution in certain countries in the region.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may contribute to its potential health benefits in treating wounds, infections, and respiratory issues.
